members.name: AreaCreate
members.signature: public RID AreaCreate()
members.summary: Creates a 3D area object in the physics server, and returns the [RID] that identifies it. The default settings for the created area include a collision layer and mask set to [code]1[/code], and [code]monitorable[/code] set to [code]false[/code]. Use [method area_add_shape] to add shapes to it, use [method area_set_transform] to set its transform, and use [method area_set_space] to add the area to a space. If you want the area to be detectable use [method area_set_monitorable].

members.name: AreaSetAreaMonitorCallback
members.signature: public void AreaSetAreaMonitorCallback(RID area, Callable callback)
members.summary: Sets the area's area monitor callback. This callback will be called when any other (shape of an) area enters or exits (a shape of) the given area, and must take the following five parameters: 1. an integer [code]status[/code]: either [constant AREA_BODY_ADDED] or [constant AREA_BODY_REMOVED] depending on whether the other area's shape entered or exited the area, 2. an [RID] [code]area_rid[/code]: the [RID] of the other area that entered or exited the area, 3. an integer [code]instance_id[/code]: the [code]ObjectID[/code] attached to the other area, 4. an integer [code]area_shape_idx[/code]: the index of the shape of the other area that entered or exited the area, 5. an integer [code]self_shape_idx[/code]: the index of the shape of the area where the other area entered or exited. By counting (or keeping track of) the shapes that enter and exit, it can be determined if an area (with all its shapes) is entering for the first time or exiting for the last time.

members.name: AreaSetMonitorCallback
members.signature: public void AreaSetMonitorCallback(RID area, Callable callback)
members.summary: Sets the area's body monitor callback. This callback will be called when any other (shape of a) body enters or exits (a shape of) the given area, and must take the following five parameters: 1. an integer [code]status[/code]: either [constant AREA_BODY_ADDED] or [constant AREA_BODY_REMOVED] depending on whether the other body shape entered or exited the area, 2. an [RID] [code]body_rid[/code]: the [RID] of the body that entered or exited the area, 3. an integer [code]instance_id[/code]: the [code]ObjectID[/code] attached to the body, 4. an integer [code]body_shape_idx[/code]: the index of the shape of the body that entered or exited the area, 5. an integer [code]self_shape_idx[/code]: the index of the shape of the area where the body entered or exited. By counting (or keeping track of) the shapes that enter and exit, it can be determined if a body (with all its shapes) is entering for the first time or exiting for the last time.

members.name: BodyApplyCentralForce
members.signature: public void BodyApplyCentralForce(RID body, Vector3 force)
members.summary: Applies a directional force without affecting rotation. A force is time dependent and meant to be applied every physics update. This is equivalent to using [method body_apply_force] at the body's center of mass.

members.name: BodyApplyCentralImpulse
members.signature: public void BodyApplyCentralImpulse(RID body, Vector3 impulse)
members.summary: Applies a directional impulse without affecting rotation. An impulse is time-independent! Applying an impulse every frame would result in a framerate-dependent force. For this reason, it should only be used when simulating one-time impacts (use the "_force" functions otherwise). This is equivalent to using [method body_apply_impulse] at the body's center of mass.

members.name: BodyApplyForce
members.signature: public void BodyApplyForce(RID body, Vector3 force, Vector3 position)
members.summary: Applies a positioned force to the body. A force is time dependent and meant to be applied every physics update. [param position] is the offset from the body origin in global coordinates.

members.name: BodyApplyImpulse
members.signature: public void BodyApplyImpulse(RID body, Vector3 impulse, Vector3 position)
members.summary: Applies a positioned impulse to the body. An impulse is time-independent! Applying an impulse every frame would result in a framerate-dependent force. For this reason, it should only be used when simulating one-time impacts (use the "_force" functions otherwise). [param position] is the offset from the body origin in global coordinates.

members.name: BodyApplyTorque
members.signature: public void BodyApplyTorque(RID body, Vector3 torque)
members.summary: Applies a rotational force without affecting position. A force is time dependent and meant to be applied every physics update.

members.name: BodyApplyTorqueImpulse
members.signature: public void BodyApplyTorqueImpulse(RID body, Vector3 impulse)
members.summary: Applies a rotational impulse to the body without affecting the position. An impulse is time-independent! Applying an impulse every frame would result in a framerate-dependent force. For this reason, it should only be used when simulating one-time impacts (use the "_force" functions otherwise).

members.name: BodySetForceIntegrationCallback
members.signature: public void BodySetForceIntegrationCallback(RID body, Callable callable, Variant userdata)
members.summary: Sets the body's custom force integration callback function to [param callable]. Use an empty [Callable] ([code skip-lint]Callable()[/code]) to clear the custom callback. The function [param callable] will be called every physics tick, before the standard force integration (see [method body_set_omit_force_integration]). It can be used for example to update the body's linear and angular velocity based on contact with other bodies. If [param userdata] is not [code]null[/code], the function [param callable] must take the following two parameters: 1. [code]state[/code]: a [PhysicsDirectBodyState3D], used to retrieve and modify the body's state, 2. [code skip-lint]userdata[/code]: a [Variant]; its value will be the [param userdata] passed into this method. If [param userdata] is [code]null[/code], then [param callable] must take only the [code]state[/code] parameter.

members.signature: public void BodySetMaxContactsReported(RID body, int amount)
members.summary: Sets the maximum contacts to report. Bodies can keep a log of the contacts with other bodies. This is enabled by setting the maximum number of contacts reported to a number greater than 0.

members.name: BodySetOmitForceIntegration
members.signature: public void BodySetOmitForceIntegration(RID body, bool enable)
members.summary: Sets whether the body omits the standard force integration. If [param enable] is [code]true[/code], the body will not automatically use applied forces, torques, and damping to update the body's linear and angular velocity. In this case, [method body_set_force_integration_callback] can be used to manually update the linear and angular velocity instead. This method is called when the property [member RigidBody3D.custom_integrator] is set.

members.name: ShapeGetData
members.signature: public Variant ShapeGetData(RID shape)
members.summary: Returns the shape data that configures the shape, such as the half-extents of a box or the triangles of a concave (trimesh) shape. See [method shape_set_data] for the precise format of this data in each case.

members.name: ShapeGetMargin
members.signature: public float ShapeGetMargin(RID shape)
members.summary: Returns the collision margin for the shape. [b]Note:[/b] This is not used in Godot Physics, so will always return [code]0[/code].

members.name: ShapeGetType
members.signature: public int ShapeGetType(RID shape)
members.summary: Returns the shape's type.

members.name: ShapeSetData
members.signature: public void ShapeSetData(RID shape, Variant data)
members.summary: Sets the shape data that configures the shape. The [param data] to be passed depends on the shape's type (see [method shape_get_type]): - [constant SHAPE_WORLD_BOUNDARY]: a [Plane], - [constant SHAPE_SEPARATION_RAY]: a dictionary containing the key [code]"length"[/code] with a [float] value and the key [code]"slide_on_slope"[/code] with a [bool] value, - [constant SHAPE_SPHERE]: a [float] that is the radius of the sphere, - [constant SHAPE_BOX]: a [Vector3] containing the half-extents of the box, - [constant SHAPE_CAPSULE]: a dictionary containing the keys [code]"height"[/code] and [code]"radius"[/code] with [float] values, - [constant SHAPE_CYLINDER]: a dictionary containing the keys [code]"height"[/code] and [code]"radius"[/code] with [float] values, - [constant SHAPE_CONVEX_POLYGON]: a [PackedVector3Array] of points defining a convex polygon (the shape will be the convex hull of the points), - [constant SHAPE_CONCAVE_POLYGON]: a dictionary containing the key [code]"faces"[/code] with a [PackedVector3Array] value (with a length divisible by 3, so that each 3-tuple of points forms a face) and the key [code]"backface_collision"[/code] with a [bool] value, - [constant SHAPE_HEIGHTMAP]: a dictionary containing the keys [code]"width"[/code] and [code]"depth"[/code] with [int] values, and the key [code]"heights"[/code] with a value that is a packed array of [float]s of length [code]width * depth[/code] (that is a [PackedFloat32Array], or a [PackedFloat64Array] if Godot was compiled with the [code]precision=double[/code] option), and optionally the keys [code]"min_height"[/code] and [code]"max_height"[/code] with [float] values, - [constant SHAPE_SOFT_BODY]: the input [param data] is ignored and this method has no effect, - [constant SHAPE_CUSTOM]: the input [param data] is interpreted by a custom physics server, if it supports custom shapes.

values.name: AREA_BODY_ADDED
values.summary: The value of the first parameter and area callback function receives, when an object enters one of its shapes.

values.name: AREA_BODY_REMOVED
values.summary: The value of the first parameter and area callback function receives, when an object exits one of its shapes.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP
values.summary: Constant to set/get the angular damping factor of an area.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP_OVERRIDE_MODE
values.summary: Constant to set/get angular damping override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY
values.summary: Constant to set/get gravity strength in an area.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_IS_POINT
values.summary: Constant to set/get whether the gravity vector of an area is a direction, or a center point.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_OVERRIDE_MODE
values.summary: Constant to set/get gravity override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_POINT_UNIT_DISTANCE
values.summary: Constant to set/get the distance at which the gravity strength is equal to the gravity controlled by [constant AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY]. For example, on a planet 100 meters in radius with a surface gravity of 4.0 m/s², set the gravity to 4.0 and the unit distance to 100.0. The gravity will have falloff according to the inverse square law, so in the example, at 200 meters from the center the gravity will be 1.0 m/s² (twice the distance, 1/4th the gravity), at 50 meters it will be 16.0 m/s² (half the distance, 4x the gravity), and so on. The above is true only when the unit distance is a positive number. When this is set to 0.0, the gravity will be constant regardless of distance.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_VECTOR
values.summary: Constant to set/get gravity vector/center in an area.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP
values.summary: Constant to set/get the linear damping factor of an area.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP_OVERRIDE_MODE
values.summary: Constant to set/get linear damping override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_PRIORITY
values.summary: Constant to set/get the priority (order of processing) of an area.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_ATTENUATION_FACTOR
values.summary: Constant to set/get the exponential rate at which wind force decreases with distance from its origin.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_DIRECTION
values.summary: Constant to set/get the 3D vector that specifies the direction in which an area-specific wind blows.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_FORCE_MAGNITUDE
values.summary: Constant to set/get the magnitude of area-specific wind force. This wind force only applies to [SoftBody3D] nodes. Other physics bodies are currently not affected by wind.

values.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_SOURCE
values.summary: Constant to set/get the 3D vector that specifies the origin from which an area-specific wind blows.

values.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE
values.summary: This area adds its gravity/damp values to whatever has been calculated so far. This way, many overlapping areas can combine their physics to make interesting effects.

values.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE_REPLACE
values.summary: This area adds its gravity/damp values to whatever has been calculated so far. Then stops taking into account the rest of the areas, even the default one.

values.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_DISABLED
values.summary: This area does not affect gravity/damp. These are generally areas that exist only to detect collisions, and objects entering or exiting them.

values.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE
values.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damp, even the default one, and stops taking into account the rest of the areas.

values.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE_COMBINE
values.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damp calculated so far, but keeps calculating the rest of the areas, down to the default one.

values.name: BODY_DAMP_MODE_COMBINE
values.summary: The body's damping value is added to any value set in areas or the default value.

values.name: BODY_DAMP_MODE_REPLACE
values.summary: The body's damping value replaces any value set in areas or the default value.

values.name: BODY_MODE_KINEMATIC
values.summary: Constant for kinematic bodies. In this mode, a body can be only moved by user code and collides with other bodies along its path.

values.name: BODY_MODE_RIGID
values.summary: Constant for rigid bodies. In this mode, a body can be pushed by other bodies and has forces applied.

values.name: BODY_MODE_RIGID_LINEAR
values.summary: Constant for linear rigid bodies. In this mode, a body can not rotate, and only its linear velocity is affected by external forces.

values.name: BODY_MODE_STATIC
values.summary: Constant for static bodies. In this mode, a body can be only moved by user code and doesn't collide with other bodies along its path when moved.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's angular damping factor.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP_MODE
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's angular damping mode. See [enum BodyDampMode] for possible values.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_BOUNCE
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's bounce factor.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_CENTER_OF_MASS
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's center of mass position in the body's local coordinate system.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_FRICTION
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's friction.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_GRAVITY_SCALE
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's gravity multiplier.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_INERTIA
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's inertia.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's linear damping factor.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP_MODE
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's linear damping mode. See [enum BodyDampMode] for possible values.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_MASS
values.summary: Constant to set/get a body's mass.

values.name: BODY_PARAM_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum BodyParameter] enum.

values.name: BODY_STATE_ANGULAR_VELOCITY
values.summary: Constant to set/get the current angular velocity of the body.

values.name: BODY_STATE_CAN_SLEEP
values.summary: Constant to set/get whether the body can sleep.

values.name: BODY_STATE_LINEAR_VELOCITY
values.summary: Constant to set/get the current linear velocity of the body.

values.name: BODY_STATE_SLEEPING
values.summary: Constant to sleep/wake up a body, or to get whether it is sleeping.

values.name: BODY_STATE_TRANSFORM
values.summary: Constant to set/get the current transform matrix of the body.

values.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_BIAS
values.summary: The speed with which the swing or twist will take place. The higher, the faster. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_RELAXATION
values.summary: Defines, how fast the swing- and twist-speed-difference on both sides gets synced. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_SOFTNESS
values.summary: The ease with which the Joint3D twists, if it's too low, it takes more force to twist the joint. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_SWING_SPAN
values.summary: Swing is rotation from side to side, around the axis perpendicular to the twist axis. The swing span defines, how much rotation will not get corrected along the swing axis. Could be defined as looseness in the [ConeTwistJoint3D]. If below 0.05, this behavior is locked.

values.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_TWIST_SPAN
values.summary: Twist is the rotation around the twist axis, this value defined how far the joint can twist. Twist is locked if below 0.05.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_FLAG_ENABLE_ANGULAR_LIMIT
values.summary: If set, rotational motion is possible.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_FLAG_ENABLE_LINEAR_LIMIT
values.summary: If set, linear motion is possible within the given limits.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_FLAG_ENABLE_LINEAR_MOTOR
values.summary: If set, there is a linear motor on this axis that targets a specific velocity.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_FLAG_ENABLE_MOTOR
values.summary: If set, there is a rotational motor across these axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum G6DOFJointAxisFlag] enum.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_DAMPING
values.summary: The amount of rotational damping across the axes. The lower, the more damping occurs. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_ERP
values.summary: When correcting the crossing of limits in rotation across the axes, this error tolerance factor defines how much the correction gets slowed down. The lower, the slower. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_FORCE_LIMIT
values.summary: The maximum amount of force that can occur, when rotating around the axes. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_LIMIT_SOFTNESS
values.summary: A factor that gets multiplied onto all rotations across the axes. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_LOWER_LIMIT
values.summary: The minimum rotation in negative direction to break loose and rotate around the axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_MOTOR_FORCE_LIMIT
values.summary: Maximum acceleration for the motor at the axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_MOTOR_TARGET_VELOCITY
values.summary: Target speed for the motor at the axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_RESTITUTION
values.summary: The amount of rotational restitution across the axes. The lower, the more restitution occurs. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_UPPER_LIMIT
values.summary: The minimum rotation in positive direction to break loose and rotate around the axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_DAMPING
values.summary: The amount of damping that happens at the linear motion across the axes. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_LIMIT_SOFTNESS
values.summary: A factor that gets applied to the movement across the axes. The lower, the slower the movement. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_LOWER_LIMIT
values.summary: The minimum difference between the pivot points' axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_MOTOR_FORCE_LIMIT
values.summary: The maximum force that the linear motor can apply while trying to reach the target velocity.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_MOTOR_TARGET_VELOCITY
values.summary: The velocity that the joint's linear motor will attempt to reach.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_RESTITUTION
values.summary: The amount of restitution on the axes movement. The lower, the more velocity-energy gets lost. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_UPPER_LIMIT
values.summary: The maximum difference between the pivot points' axes.

values.name: G6DOF_JOINT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum G6DOFJointAxisParam] enum.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_FLAG_ENABLE_MOTOR
values.summary: If [code]true[/code], a motor turns the Hinge.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_FLAG_USE_LIMIT
values.summary: If [code]true[/code], the Hinge has a maximum and a minimum rotation.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_BIAS
values.summary: The speed with which the two bodies get pulled together when they move in different directions. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_LIMIT_BIAS
values.summary: The speed with which the rotation across the axis perpendicular to the hinge gets corrected. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_LIMIT_LOWER
values.summary: The minimum rotation across the Hinge.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_LIMIT_RELAXATION
values.summary: The lower this value, the more the rotation gets slowed down. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_LIMIT_SOFTNESS
values.summary: [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_LIMIT_UPPER
values.summary: The maximum rotation across the Hinge.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_MOTOR_MAX_IMPULSE
values.summary: Maximum acceleration for the motor.

values.name: HINGE_JOINT_MOTOR_TARGET_VELOCITY
values.summary: Target speed for the motor.

values.name: JOINT_TYPE_6DOF
values.summary: The [Joint3D] is a [Generic6DOFJoint3D].

values.name: JOINT_TYPE_CONE_TWIST
values.summary: The [Joint3D] is a [ConeTwistJoint3D].

values.name: JOINT_TYPE_HINGE
values.summary: The [Joint3D] is a [HingeJoint3D].

values.name: JOINT_TYPE_MAX

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_ANGULAR_VELOCITY_SLEEP_THRESHOLD
values.summary: Constant to set/get the threshold angular velocity of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after the time given. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_LINEAR_VELOCITY_SLEEP_THRESHOLD
values.summary: Constant to set/get the threshold linear velocity of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after the time given. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_TIME_TO_SLEEP
values.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum time of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after this time.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_DEFAULT_BIAS
values.summary: Constant to set/get the default solver bias for all physics contacts. A solver bias is a factor controlling how much two objects "rebound", after overlapping, to avoid leaving them in that state because of numerical imprecision.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_MAX_ALLOWED_PENETRATION
values.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a shape can penetrate another shape before it is considered a collision.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_MAX_SEPARATION
values.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a shape can be from another before they are considered separated and the contact is discarded.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_RECYCLE_RADIUS
values.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a pair of bodies has to move before their collision status has to be recalculated.

values.name: SPACE_PARAM_SOLVER_ITERATIONS
values.summary: Constant to set/get the number of solver iterations for contacts and constraints. The greater the number of iterations, the more accurate the collisions and constraints will be. However, a greater number of iterations requires more CPU power, which can decrease performance.

constants.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_SWING_SPAN
constants.summary: Swing is rotation from side to side, around the axis perpendicular to the twist axis. The swing span defines, how much rotation will not get corrected along the swing axis. Could be defined as looseness in the [ConeTwistJoint3D]. If below 0.05, this behavior is locked.

constants.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_TWIST_SPAN
constants.summary: Twist is the rotation around the twist axis, this value defined how far the joint can twist. Twist is locked if below 0.05.

constants.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_BIAS
constants.summary: The speed with which the swing or twist will take place. The higher, the faster. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_SOFTNESS
constants.summary: The ease with which the Joint3D twists, if it's too low, it takes more force to twist the joint. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: CONE_TWIST_JOINT_RELAXATION
constants.summary: Defines, how fast the swing- and twist-speed-difference on both sides gets synced. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_RESTITUTION
constants.summary: The amount of restitution on the axes movement. The lower, the more velocity-energy gets lost. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: G6DOF_JOINT_LINEAR_DAMPING

constants.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_LIMIT_SOFTNESS

constants.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_DAMPING
constants.summary: The amount of rotational damping across the axes. The lower, the more damping occurs. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: G6DOF_JOINT_ANGULAR_RESTITUTION

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_OVERRIDE_MODE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get gravity override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY
constants.summary: Constant to set/get gravity strength in an area.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_VECTOR
constants.summary: Constant to set/get gravity vector/center in an area.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_IS_POINT
constants.summary: Constant to set/get whether the gravity vector of an area is a direction, or a center point.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY_POINT_UNIT_DISTANCE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the distance at which the gravity strength is equal to the gravity controlled by [constant AREA_PARAM_GRAVITY]. For example, on a planet 100 meters in radius with a surface gravity of 4.0 m/s², set the gravity to 4.0 and the unit distance to 100.0. The gravity will have falloff according to the inverse square law, so in the example, at 200 meters from the center the gravity will be 1.0 m/s² (twice the distance, 1/4th the gravity), at 50 meters it will be 16.0 m/s² (half the distance, 4x the gravity), and so on. The above is true only when the unit distance is a positive number. When this is set to 0.0, the gravity will be constant regardless of distance.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP_OVERRIDE_MODE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get linear damping override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the linear damping factor of an area.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP_OVERRIDE_MODE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get angular damping override mode in an area. See [enum AreaSpaceOverrideMode] for possible values.

constants.summary: Constant to set/get the angular damping factor of an area.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_PRIORITY
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the priority (order of processing) of an area.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_FORCE_MAGNITUDE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the magnitude of area-specific wind force. This wind force only applies to [SoftBody3D] nodes. Other physics bodies are currently not affected by wind.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_SOURCE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the 3D vector that specifies the origin from which an area-specific wind blows.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_DIRECTION
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the 3D vector that specifies the direction in which an area-specific wind blows.

constants.name: AREA_PARAM_WIND_ATTENUATION_FACTOR
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the exponential rate at which wind force decreases with distance from its origin.

constants.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_DISABLED
constants.summary: This area does not affect gravity/damp. These are generally areas that exist only to detect collisions, and objects entering or exiting them.

constants.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE
constants.summary: This area adds its gravity/damp values to whatever has been calculated so far. This way, many overlapping areas can combine their physics to make interesting effects.

constants.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE_REPLACE
constants.summary: This area adds its gravity/damp values to whatever has been calculated so far. Then stops taking into account the rest of the areas, even the default one.

constants.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE
constants.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damp, even the default one, and stops taking into account the rest of the areas.

constants.name: AREA_SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE_COMBINE
constants.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damp calculated so far, but keeps calculating the rest of the areas, down to the default one.

constants.name: BODY_MODE_STATIC
constants.summary: Constant for static bodies. In this mode, a body can be only moved by user code and doesn't collide with other bodies along its path when moved.

constants.name: BODY_MODE_KINEMATIC
constants.summary: Constant for kinematic bodies. In this mode, a body can be only moved by user code and collides with other bodies along its path.

constants.name: BODY_MODE_RIGID
constants.summary: Constant for rigid bodies. In this mode, a body can be pushed by other bodies and has forces applied.

constants.name: BODY_MODE_RIGID_LINEAR
constants.summary: Constant for linear rigid bodies. In this mode, a body can not rotate, and only its linear velocity is affected by external forces.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_BOUNCE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's bounce factor.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_FRICTION
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's friction.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_MASS
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's mass.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_INERTIA
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's inertia.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_CENTER_OF_MASS
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's center of mass position in the body's local coordinate system.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_GRAVITY_SCALE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's gravity multiplier.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP_MODE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's linear damping mode. See [enum BodyDampMode] for possible values.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP_MODE
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's angular damping mode. See [enum BodyDampMode] for possible values.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_LINEAR_DAMP
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's linear damping factor.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_ANGULAR_DAMP
constants.summary: Constant to set/get a body's angular damping factor.

constants.name: BODY_PARAM_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum BodyParameter] enum.

constants.name: BODY_DAMP_MODE_COMBINE
constants.summary: The body's damping value is added to any value set in areas or the default value.

constants.name: BODY_DAMP_MODE_REPLACE
constants.summary: The body's damping value replaces any value set in areas or the default value.

constants.name: BODY_STATE_TRANSFORM
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the current transform matrix of the body.

constants.name: BODY_STATE_LINEAR_VELOCITY
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the current linear velocity of the body.

constants.name: BODY_STATE_ANGULAR_VELOCITY
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the current angular velocity of the body.

constants.name: BODY_STATE_SLEEPING
constants.summary: Constant to sleep/wake up a body, or to get whether it is sleeping.

constants.name: BODY_STATE_CAN_SLEEP
constants.summary: Constant to set/get whether the body can sleep.

constants.name: AREA_BODY_ADDED
constants.summary: The value of the first parameter and area callback function receives, when an object enters one of its shapes.

constants.name: AREA_BODY_REMOVED
constants.summary: The value of the first parameter and area callback function receives, when an object exits one of its shapes.

constants.name: INFO_ACTIVE_OBJECTS
constants.summary: Constant to get the number of objects that are not sleeping.

constants.name: INFO_COLLISION_PAIRS
constants.summary: Constant to get the number of possible collisions.

constants.name: INFO_ISLAND_COUNT
constants.summary: Constant to get the number of space regions where a collision could occur.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_RECYCLE_RADIUS
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a pair of bodies has to move before their collision status has to be recalculated.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_MAX_SEPARATION
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a shape can be from another before they are considered separated and the contact is discarded.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_MAX_ALLOWED_PENETRATION
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum distance a shape can penetrate another shape before it is considered a collision.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_CONTACT_DEFAULT_BIAS
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the default solver bias for all physics contacts. A solver bias is a factor controlling how much two objects "rebound", after overlapping, to avoid leaving them in that state because of numerical imprecision.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_LINEAR_VELOCITY_SLEEP_THRESHOLD
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the threshold linear velocity of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after the time given. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_ANGULAR_VELOCITY_SLEEP_THRESHOLD
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the threshold angular velocity of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after the time given. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using GodotPhysics3D. This parameter is ignored when using Jolt Physics.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_BODY_TIME_TO_SLEEP
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the maximum time of activity. A body marked as potentially inactive for both linear and angular velocity will be put to sleep after this time.

constants.name: SPACE_PARAM_SOLVER_ITERATIONS
constants.summary: Constant to set/get the number of solver iterations for contacts and constraints. The greater the number of iterations, the more accurate the collisions and constraints will be. However, a greater number of iterations requires more CPU power, which can decrease performance.
